mechanism_text_snippets: The general reaction is attack of hydroxide on the aldehyde, while the terminal aldehyde hydride is transferred to the sulfido ligand of Mo. There are two half reactions that occur in the enzyme which are independent in as much as they can be uncoupled under certain conditions. In the oxidative half reaction the Fe (II) centre binds to dioxygen, acting as a nucleophile thus becoming oxidised to Fe (III). This creates a peroxide radica...

CobH is one of a few known enzymes that catalyse pericyclic reactions. His 43 protonates the nitrogen of the pyrrolenine C ring of precorrin-8x, which triggers a suprafacial [1,5]-sigmatropic rearrangement; the methyl group passes from C-11 to C-12 in a single concerted step, with the double bond...

The reaction occurs in two steps, the first reaction of which only occurs in the presence of the bound cognate tRNA molecule. This kinase first phosphorylates His45, the phosphate group is then passed on to the nucleophilic Asp of the CheY substrate.
The enzyme catalyses the biological equivalent of the Pictet-Spengler reaction, a reaction used to form synthetic equivalents of indole alkaloids of pharmaceutical interest.

SAM is activated by the [Fe4S4]-AdoMet cluster to produce the methionine substrate and the 5'-deoxyadenosyl radical (5'-dA).
